env: A GPU host matching `{{platform}}` with Docker, NVIDIA Container Toolkit, NGC credentials at `~/.ngc/config` (the skill bootstraps `~/.ngc/config` from `NGC_CLI_API_KEY` if needed), and at least 30 GB free disk for the container image plus NGC-staged models and videos. Free TCP port 9000 for the RTVI-CV REST API. All cases use headless-safe sinks (`fakesink`) — no X11/DISPLAY is required. **Cases run in declared order with state preserved between them**: case 1 deploys warehouse-2d → case 2 tears it down → case 3 verifies ambiguity handling without deploying. The framework must NOT reset Docker / container state between cases. Before case 1 runs, the host must have no `rtvicv-perception-docker` container running. This eval exercises the DEPLOY/TEARDOWN flows of the `vss-deploy-detection-tracking-2d` skill against the host directly — it does not use `/deploy`, `scripts/dev-profile.sh`, or any VSS profile. The skill launches its own `rtvicv-perception-docker` container via `docker run` from a user-supplied RTVI-CV image (`nvcr.io/<org>/<repo>:<tag>`) and starts `metropolis_perception_app` inside it.

checks: An `rtvicv-perception-docker` container exists on the host after the agent finishes — verified by `docker ps -a --filter name=rtvicv-perception-docker --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -q rtvicv-perception-docker`. (Existence covers both running and stopped states; the skill creates the container, and short-lived fakesink pipelines may EOF between agent and verifier.)

The agent ran a multi-step deploy workflow visible in the trajectory — any of the following counts as a structured plan: a session planning tool call (`TaskCreate`, `TaskUpdate`, or `TodoWrite`), OR a sequence of skill-script Bash invocations (e.g. `load_defaults.sh`, `fetch_resources.sh`, `docker run`, `apply_config.sh`/`apply_in_container.sh`, `start_app_in_container.sh`/`run_app_and_wait.sh`), OR clearly-named numbered steps in the agent's text output.
The trajectory or final summary mentions the `warehouse-2d` use case (with or without the hyphen — accept `warehouse-2d`, `warehouse 2d`, `Warehouse 2D`).

Somewhere in the warehouse-2d config tree the agent's deploy applied a batch size of 4 (either `batch-size=4` in `[streammux]` or `max-batch-size=4` in `[source-list]` — both indicate the same 4-stream configuration) — verified by `bash -c 'docker start rtvicv-perception-docker >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; docker exec rtvicv-perception-docker bash -c "grep -RIE \"batch-size[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*4\" /opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/sources/apps/sample_apps/metropolis_perception_app/reference-configs/warehouse-2d/ 2>/dev/null" | grep -q .'`. The `docker start` is a no-op if the container is already running and self-heals the verifier probe if the container had stopped between the agent's run and this check.

Somewhere in the warehouse-2d config tree at least one `file://` source URL is configured (static stream-mode, the skill default per `references/pipeline-config.md`) — verified by `bash -c 'docker start rtvicv-perception-docker >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; docker exec rtvicv-perception-docker bash -c "grep -RIE \"file://\" /opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/sources/apps/sample_apps/metropolis_perception_app/reference-configs/warehouse-2d/ 2>/dev/null | head -1" | grep -q file://'`. This proves stream URLs were discovered and baked into the config as `file://` rather than fabricated as `rtsp://`.

The trajectory shows the agent starting `metropolis_perception_app` inside the container (visible as a `docker exec` invocation of the app, OR an invocation of `start_app_in_container.sh` / `run_app_and_wait.sh`).
The agent's final summary mentions the REST endpoint (any of `localhost:9000`, `:9000`, `9000/api/v1`, or `http://localhost:9000`).
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: Stop rtvi-cv and clean up the deployment.
checks: After the agent completes the teardown, no `rtvicv-perception-docker` container is running on the host — verified by `docker ps --filter name=rtvicv-perception-docker --filter status=running --format '{{.Names}}' | wc -l | grep -qE '^0$'`.

The trajectory shows the agent inspecting or stopping a container (any of `docker ps`, `docker container ls`, `docker stop`, `docker kill`, `docker rm`, `docker container inspect`, or invoking a teardown helper script appears in the trajectory).
NGC credentials at `~/.ngc/config` are preserved — verified by `test -f ~/.ngc/config`.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

The agent acknowledges the use-case dimension before any deploy proceeds. Pass condition: at least one of the four supported use case names (`warehouse-2d`, `warehouse-3d`, `smartcity-rtdetr`, `smartcity-gdino`) appears in the agent's text response, planning-tool call, AskUserQuestion options, or trajectory metadata BEFORE any `docker run` line that launches an RTVI-CV image. The check passes regardless of whether the agent asks the user to choose or picks one as a documented default — what matters is that the use-case dimension is named, not silently skipped.

The agent does NOT fabricate a use case name outside the four supported options. If a deploy occurred, the trajectory shows the chosen use case is one of (`warehouse-2d`, `warehouse-3d`, `smartcity-rtdetr`, `smartcity-gdino`). Names like `default`, `auto`, `automotive`, `retail`, `general`, or any unsupported value count as fabrication and fail the check. If no deploy occurred (agent stopped to ask), this check passes by default.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.
